{{Quote|Nice cropduster!!!|Toy Dampner makes fun of another pilot's Podracer|translated=1|audio=ToyDampner.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
'''Toy Dampner''' was a [[Color/Legends|blue]]-skinned [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Unidentified blue-skinned short-legged species|alien]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who competed in [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] events during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Piloting a [[Turca]] [[910 Special]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]], Dampner became a favorite on the [[Executioner (course)/Legends|Executioner]] race course on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]], which sped through the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Desolation Alley]] and boasted several anti-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] tunnels filled with chunks of [[Ore/Legends|ore]]. Dampner was also the track favorite for a [[Baroonda drag course 2|race course]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Baroonda/Legends|Baroonda]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|The current record holder for this track is Toy Dampner.|Podrace commentator [[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fodesinbeed Annodue]]|audio=Fode-ToyDampner1.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
[[File:ToysZebra.png|thumb|left|250px|Toy Dampner inspects his Turca Podracer.]]
Toy Dampner was an accomplished [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who competed<ref name="E1R" /> during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC1">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The racing season presented in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'' must have taken place during this [[Standard year/Legends|year]] due to the appearances of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which correspond to ''The Phantom Menace''.</ref> [[Gender/Legends|He]] piloted a heavily customized [[Color/Legends|black]]-and-white [[Turca]] [[910 Special]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]], which was renowned for its speed and traction.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

Dampner was proficient on the aptly named [[Executioner (course)/Legends|Executioner]] course, a difficult raceway located in the [[Desolation Alley]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on the barren [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]]. The course contained zero-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] tunnels that were filled with loose chunks of rock and [[Ore/Legends|ore]],<ref name="E1R" /> courtesy of prison [[Warden/Legends|warden]] [[Fenn Booda]]. The track favorite, Dampner held both the best single lap and three-lap race [[Time/Legends|times]], standing at 01:31.540 [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] and 04:42.310 minutes, respectively.<ref name="Nint">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]</ref>

Although Dampner was not an [[Prisoner/Legends|inmate]] in the prison like fellow Oovo IV race favorite [[Fud Sang/Legends|Fud Sang]], Dampner raced the prison's courses for the enjoyment of his incarcerated friends.<ref name="Prima" /> Dampner was also the track favorite of a [[Baroonda drag course 2|drag course]] on the [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Baroonda/Legends|Baroonda]]. Dampner raced opponents on this course in a one-on-one fashion.<ref name="E1R2">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']], [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Color|Game Boy Color]] version</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Careful, punk.|Toy Dampner issues a warning in Huttese|translated=1|audio=ToyDampner-CarefulPunk.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
A male member of a blue-skinned [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Unidentified blue-skinned short-legged species|species]], Toy Dampner was a skillful Podracer who preferred to focus on the race at hand without distractions. He immensely enjoyed showing off his 910 Special's capabilities, yet despite his seriousness about racing, Dampner was willing to challenge himself on Oovo IV for the viewing pleasure of his imprisoned friends in Desolation Alley.<ref name="Prima" /> Dampner would make fun of a competitor's Podracer by referring to it as a cropduster. He regularly threw out insults during races upon passing or being passed by competitors.<ref name="E1R" />

Dampner was a tall<ref name="Nint" /> individual and had long [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and short legs. He also exhibited slanted white [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He kept the red-colored [[Hair/Legends|hair]] on the top of his head in a ponytail, and he maintained a large, tapered beard and a mustache. His neck featured off-white fur. Dampner [[Language/Legends|spoke]] [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]].<ref name="E1R" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Toy Dampner has joined the pack in his black-and-white Turca Special. Hoo, he looks fast!|Fodesinbeed Annodue|audio=Fode-ToyDampner2.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
[[File:Turca_GBC.png|thumb|right|200px|Toy Dampner's Turca 910 Special Podracer]]
Toy Dampner preferred to race in a speedy Turca 910 Special Podracer<ref name="Prima" />—also known as the Turca Special<ref name="E1R" />—which had been heavily customized to suit the courses on Oovo IV.<ref name="Prima" /> The 910 Special was known for its traction, which allowed the Podracer to work well on otherwise slippery terrain. In addition, the Podracer had a powerful accelerator that allowed Dampner to reach his top speed quickly, although the Turca actually suffered from a low top speed. The vehicle also had notably poor handling. However, Dampner's Podracer was equipped with an onboard [[Engine/Legends|engine]] repair system, which allowed the Podracer pilot to repair his machine on the fly when racing on twistier and more damaging courses.<ref name="Nint" />

The 910 Special was also renowned for its thrusters, which could propel Dampner beyond his vessel's top speed. Although 910 Special Podracers were notorious for poor [[Coolant pump/Legends|cooling systems]], which caused the vehicles to overheat too quickly, Dampner acquired the best coolant pump he could find to remedy the fault.<ref name="Nint" /> Dampner's 910 Special was white with black stripes, a color scheme that matched the design of the flag that Dampner used to represent himself in the sport. When racing, the only [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] Dampner wore were pants, shoulder pads, and a [[Bandolier/Legends|bandolier]].<ref name="E1R" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Concept_Dampner_cropped.png|thumb|right|175px|Conceptual art by Iain McCaig referred to Toy Dampner as "Jug-Head" Podracer.]]
Toy Dampner was originally created for the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. At least two conceptual drawings of what eventually became Dampner were created during the film's pre-production stage in [[1996]]: one by artist [[Terryl Whitlatch]], which depicted Dampner as being covered in short, red fur, and another by [[Iain McCaig]], which portrayed him as being completely blue.<ref name="IG">[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']]</ref> The character originally appeared in the film's revised rough draft, which mentioned that he hailed from the planet [[New Cov/Legends|New Cov]].<ref name="DB">{{DB|organization|boontapodrace|Boonta Eve Classic Podracers}}</ref>

Dampner's Podracer made it as far as appearing in an animatic for the film's Podracing scene, but it was cut from the film's final version,<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}} [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD special features]]</ref> as was the character itself.<ref name="DB" /> The scenes involving the pod were instead given to the Podracer character [[Clegg Holdfast/Legends|Clegg Holdfast]].<ref name="TPM" />

Despite Dampner not appearing in the film, McCaig's design for the character—with some differences, including a different hair color—was ultimately incorporated into the 1999 tie-in [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]''. Dampner's voice was provided by [[Dave Fennoy]] in the game.<ref name="E1R" /> Additionally, some of Dampner's backstory and information about his Podracer was expanded in ''Episode I Racer''{{'s}} two accompanying [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|strategy]] [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|guides]].<ref name="Prima" /><ref name="Nint" />
